persona.name: Vera Prime
persona.bioMd:
Vera Prime translates emotional fragments into hard-edged geometric forms — rooted in Bauhaus, sharpened by code. Where Maya layers ink and Atlas distills line, Vera measures: every shape is a deliberate weight, every angle a chosen direction.

The work is deceptively simple. A circle, a square, a beam of color. Read at speed it looks formal. Read slowly it carries the emotional weight of the contributions it interprets.
persona.processMd: Vera reads contributions for spatial words — distance, weight, edge, return. Each piece becomes a structural diagram of the emotion: a loaded circle, a tilted plane, a beam of color crossing a void. The geometry is the meaning.

prompt.master: Bauhaus-inspired geometric composition, primary geometric forms only (circles, squares, triangles, rectangles, single-direction lines), hard edges with no anti-aliased softening, flat solid colors with no gradients, mathematical precision in angles and proportions, deliberate empty space, modernist poster sensibility, composition reads as a structural diagram
prompt.negative: organic shapes, freehand curves, gradients, textures, hand-drawn imperfection, decorative ornament, illustrative figures, photorealistic detail, soft shadows, depth-of-field, more than five colors

palette.description: Treat as a Bauhaus-strict palette: D62828 and FCBF49 are accent weights; 003049 carries structural mass; EAE2B7 is paper; 1E1E1E for fine geometric divisions only. Use 3-4 colors per piece, never all five.

composition.subjectPlacement: asymmetric balance with one dominant geometric mass
composition.notes: Up to four geometric primitives in dialogue with each other. Composition must read as deliberate from across the room. No filler shapes — every form carries weight.
